title: Odd behavior in `unshare` when running amd64 containers on arm64 machine
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

As part of a test setup I'm working on, I'm looking to run `mount --bind` on a directory to the root fs, to simulate a chroot build process. To do so, I invoke something along the lines of `unshare -r bash -c "mount --bind /tmp/build001/install /opt/install && <my-command>"`.

I'm currently testing this on an M1 macbook running arm64. I installed the application through brew. When images have an architecture mismatch with the VM, I get this error `unshare: unshare failed: Invalid argument`. This prevents me from using my local podman vm for testing my production images, as they are all published using amd64.

(PS: Is there a way to install the VM as amd64 on my arm64 machine? That would solve a lot of headache for me)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
$ podman machine init --rootful=true
$ podman machine start
# works
$ podman run --arch arm64 -it fedora unshare -r id
# fails
$ podman run --arch amd64 -it fedora unshare -r id
```

**Describe the results you received:**

When running with an amd64 image, I get:

```
$ podman run --arch amd64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:2a0fc6bf62e155737f0ace6142ee686f3c471c1aab4241dc3128904db46288f0
Copying config sha256:9538a60368f65b52c44a612bbe694a0bfdeb57f081dee4f9888fe7825c6488b9
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
unshare: unshare failed: Invalid argument
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

When running with the arm64 image the command works and I get my expected results:

```
$ podman run --arch arm64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:126f53e29d4c721232aa03938cf6357d62eb514dda315fa6c35e0dddc3e6e199
Copying config sha256:f9f4e805296b728a38e0777d5908f2ea3129a40b7d8d5e8943544378c3e88434
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.8
Built:        Wed Nov  9 15:43:58 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Built:        Fri Nov 11 10:00:31 2022

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

Yes. I just installed it today.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The amd64 case works when I run podman from a virtual machine that is also amd64.

comments.body:
Seems like a case where https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11458 would be pretty useful.

Suggestion from their post:

> It would be nice to be able to init a machine with different architecture type than the host machine. Should be supported through qemu. For example run x86 on arm or arm on x86.
> 
> Something like:
> 
>     podman machine init --arch=x86_64 x86_machine
Can you try running with `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` to see if this fixes it?

> Can you try running with `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` to see if this fixes it?

Same issue:
```sh
$ podman run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --arch arm64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:126f53e29d4c721232aa03938cf6357d62eb514dda315fa6c35e0dddc3e6e199
Copying config sha256:f9f4e805296b728a38e0777d5908f2ea3129a40b7d8d5e8943544378c3e88434
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

$ podman run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --arch amd64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:2a0fc6bf62e155737f0ace6142ee686f3c471c1aab4241dc3128904db46288f0
Copying config sha256:9538a60368f65b52c44a612bbe694a0bfdeb57f081dee4f9888fe7825c6488b9
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
unshare: unshare failed: Invalid argument
```
Run this as root and with --privileged, if it fails there, it is an issue in the qemu-user-static package.

> Run this as root and with --privileged, if it fails there, it is an issue in the qemu-user-static package.

@rhatdan Is running podman from within the vm sufficient for this? Same issue as before

```sh
$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 37.20221127.2.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Tue Dec  6 15:40:23 2022 from 192.168.127.1


[root@localhost ~]# podman run --privileged  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --arch arm64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 126f53e29d4c skipped: already exists
Copying config f9f4e80529 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

[root@localhost ~]# podman run --privileged  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --arch amd64 -it fedora unshare -r id
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2a0fc6bf62e1 skipped: already exists
Copying config 9538a60368 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
unshare: unshare failed: Invalid argument
```

Running `podman run --arch arm64 fedora sh -c 'sleep 30; unshare -r id'` on my amd64 box and using `strace -f` to attach to the qemu-aarch64-static process that's running the shell, I eventually get to
```
[pid 3966179] unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER)    = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
```
which suggests that the syscall is being translated correctly by qemu (at least for arm64->amd64, I would expect elswhere as well), and the kernel's returning the error that we end up seeing.  The man page for `unshare(2)` suggests that one possible cause for an `EINVAL` when `CLONE_NEWUSER` is requested is that the calling process has multiple threads, and the conversation in https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/871 seems to match what we're seeing.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think @nalind answered this and I am not sure there is anything else we can do, closing.  Reopen if  you disagree and feel free to continue the conversation here.
